findings.finding: Task 12 concurrency test uses Barrier(2) so both callers open the same inode before either os.replace; cannot detect the broken invariant that lives in the post-replace window
findings.evidence_quote: use multiprocessing.Pool... Use a multiprocessing.Barrier(2) to maximize the race window.

findings.finding: repo_root canonicalization not threaded from Task 9 step 2 into Task 7 register(); registered allowWrite entry may be non-canonical on systems with symlinked ancestor directories

Forward-direction symlink bypass — a symlink created at lifecycle/sessions/ after cortex init runs is never rechecked, yet its target is already in allowWrite; overnight runner's --dangerously-skip-permissions makes allowWrite the sole enforcement surface
findings.evidence_quote: Revalidating the registered path after the fact — cortex init registers $(repo-root)/lifecycle/sessions/ once per invocation; it does not later detect stale entries for deleted repos

findings.finding: test_partial_failure_recovery exercises only step-5 failure; steps 2/3/4 partial-failure recovery paths untested
findings.evidence_quote: monkeypatch settings_merge.atomic_write to raise OSError('disk full') once

findings.finding: ADR-2 framing claims concurrent callers serialize such that each sees the prior writer's mutation — only true between two successful callers; failed-caller scenario leaves caller-B reading pre-failure state
findings.evidence_quote: Concurrent callers serialize through the kernel's advisory lock; each sees the prior writer's mutation.

findings.finding: --unregister against a non-git or deleted-repo path is reachable but step 1 git-repo gate fires before step 4 unregister dispatch; cleanup of deleted repos not possible via --path
findings.evidence_quote: 1. Resolve repo_root (R2 + R3 gates run here). ... 4. If --unregister: call settings_merge.unregister(repo_root) and return 0.

findings.finding: no-marker + --force branch ('R19 doesn't fire; backup + overwrite + marker') is named in handler prose but not a named test function
findings.evidence_quote: Otherwise (no marker): branch on --update (additive scaffold + marker write) / --force (R19 doesn't fire; backup + overwrite + marker)

findings.finding: no-marker + --update branch not covered by any named test; test_marker_refresh_on_update and test_update_preserves_user_edits both start from post-init state
findings.evidence_quote: Otherwise (no marker): branch on --update (additive scaffold + marker write)

findings.finding: Task 9 step 3 calls settings_merge.validate_settings(home) but Task 7/8 don't expose such a signature; ADR-3 ordering guarantee untestable if inlined into register
findings.evidence_quote: Pre-flight malformed-settings validation (R14) — call a thin settings_merge.validate_settings(home) that acquires/releases the lock quickly.

findings.finding: No test for --unregister against malformed settings despite Task 8 specifying unregister runs the same R14 validate logic
findings.evidence_quote: Mirrors register exactly: acquire flock, read, validate sandbox/filesystem types (same diagnostics as R14)

findings.finding: test_partial_failure_recovery lives in test_settings_merge.py but calls init_main; test_scaffold.py has no corresponding partial-failure test; marker-refresh side-effect on recovery is unasserted
findings.evidence_quote: Restore atomic_write behavior; call init_main with --update; assert settings now contains the entry and exit 0
